mnemonics: DIFFie and HelMAN go to the BEACH to exchange KEYS. They use a bucket (public color) to mix their secret colors (private keys) and create a shared secret color (session key) without anyone else on the beach knowing their original secrets.
keyPoints: Diffie-Hellman (DH) is a method for securely exchanging cryptographic keys over a public channel.
It allows two parties to jointly establish a shared secret key, even over an insecure connection.
Its security relies on the computational difficulty of the Discrete Logarithm Problem (DLP).
commonMistakes: Assuming Diffie-Hellman provides authentication; it is susceptible to Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks without additional authentication measures.
Confusing key exchange (DH) with encryption/decryption; DH only establishes a shared secret, which must then be used by another protocol for encryption.
